# cognito-toolkit (v3)

> ESM-only middleware family for AWS Cognito authentication & authorization in web apps. Verification is delegated to AWS's official `aws-jwt-verify` (the single runtime dependency, itself dependency-free); this package supplies the glue: Koa, Express, Fetch-style (Bun/Deno/Cloudflare Workers), and AWS Lambda ports with route guards (`isAuthenticated` / `hasGroup` / `hasScope` / `isAllowed`) and an auth-cookie convenience, a framework-free `makeGetUser` adapter (`token => payload | null`), and two `utils/` helpers that fetch and cache OAuth2 `client_credentials` access tokens. The port family mirrors dynamodb-toolkit's adapters, so the two compose without glue. Runtime targets Node 20+, latest Bun, latest Deno.

## Install

```sh
npm install cognito-toolkit
```

## Quick start (Koa; Express is identical modulo the import)

```js
import {CognitoJwtVerifier} from 'cognito-toolkit';
import {makeAuth} from 'cognito-toolkit/koa'; // or 'cognito-toolkit/express'

const verifier = CognitoJwtVerifier.create({userPoolId: 'us-east-1_AbCdef', clientId: 'my-app-client', tokenUse: 'access'});
const auth = makeAuth({verifier});

app.use(auth.getUser); // ctx.state.user (Koa) / req.user (Express) = decoded payload or null
router.get('/protected', auth.isAuthenticated, handler);
router.post('/writers', auth.hasGroup('writers'), handler);
```

CommonJS consumers use named exports via `require(esm)`: `const {makeAuth} = require('cognito-toolkit/koa')`.

## Sub-exports

- `cognito-toolkit` — `makeGetUser` (default + named) + re-exported `CognitoJwtVerifier` / `JwtVerifier` / `AlbJwtVerifier` from aws-jwt-verify.
- `cognito-toolkit/koa` — `makeAuth` (default + named): Koa middleware bundle.
- `cognito-toolkit/express` — `makeAuth` (default + named): Express middleware bundle.
- `cognito-toolkit/fetch` — `makeAuth` (default + named): Fetch handler-wrapper bundle (Bun.serve, Deno.serve, Cloudflare Workers, Hono, …).
- `cognito-toolkit/lambda` — `makeAuth` (default + named): AWS Lambda handler-wrapper bundle (API Gateway v1/v2, Function URL, ALB events auto-detected).
- `cognito-toolkit/utils/lazy-access-token` — `createLazyAccessToken` (default + named). On-demand, cached `client_credentials` access-token holder.
- `cognito-toolkit/utils/renewable-access-token` — `createRenewableAccessToken` (default + named). Timer-renewed `client_credentials` access-token holder.

## API

### `makeAuth(options)` (koa / express / fetch / lambda) `-> {getUser, isAuthenticated, hasGroup, hasScope, isAllowed, setAuthCookie, stateUserProperty?}`

Options:

- `verifier` — **required.** An aws-jwt-verify verifier, or anything with async `verify(token) -> payload` that throws on invalid tokens. Configure pools / `clientId` / `tokenUse` / `customJwtCheck` on the verifier.
- `authHeader` — header carrying the bare token (no `Bearer` stripping; strip in a custom `source`). Falsy disables. Default `'Authorization'`.
- `authCookie` — cookie carrying the token + target of the auth-cookie helpers. Falsy disables both. Default `'auth'`. Express reads `req.cookies` (needs cookie-parser).
- `source` — custom token source `ctx|req => token | null`; overrides header/cookie lookups.
- `setAuthCookieOptions` — when set (any object, `{}` fine), authenticated requests refresh the auth cookie automatically.
- `stateUserProperty` — user property on `ctx.state` / `req`. Default `'user'`.
- `throwOnError` — verification failures throw (aws-jwt-verify error classes) instead of yielding an anonymous request. Default `false`.

Bundle members:

- `getUser` — middleware: authenticates every request; user payload (+ `_token`, bound `setAuthCookie`) or `null` lands on the state property.
- `isAuthenticated` — guard: 401 without a user.
- `hasGroup(group)` / `hasScope(scope)` — guards: 401 without a user, 403 without the `cognito:groups` membership / `scope` claim entry.
- `isAllowed(validator)` — guard with `(ctx|req, groups, scopes) => boolean | Promise<boolean>`; falsy → 401 (anonymous) / 403 (authenticated).
- `setAuthCookie(ctx, opts?)` / `setAuthCookie(req, res, opts?)` — persist the current token into the auth cookie (skipped when already there). Expires with the token; domain defaults to the request hostname.

Fetch / Lambda differences (immutable request / pristine event — no state property):

- `getUser(request | event)` is a memoized async lookup (one verification per request; WeakMap-backed) instead of a middleware.
- Guards wrap handlers — `auth.isAuthenticated(handler)`, `auth.hasGroup(g)(handler)` — passing extra args through (Bun `server`, Cloudflare `env`/`ctx`, Lambda `context`); denials are a `Response` (fetch) or `{statusCode: 401|403}` envelope (lambda, ALB multi-value mode mirrored).
- `setAuthCookie(request, response, opts?)` / `setAuthCookie(event, result, opts?)` is async and returns the response/result to use; automatic refresh (via `setAuthCookieOptions`) applies on guarded responses. Cookies serialized in-house: `Path=/`, `HttpOnly` by default.
- Lambda events: API Gateway v1/v2/Function URL/ALB auto-detected; headers read case-insensitively; v2 `cookies` array supported. Behind API Gateway proper prefer the built-in Cognito/JWT authorizer; this port's niche is Function URLs, ALB (`AlbJwtVerifier`), local bridges.

### `makeGetUser(verifier, options?) -> (token) => Promise<payload | null>`

Framework-free core (default export of `cognito-toolkit`). Absent token or failed verification → `null`; `options.throwOnError` rethrows verification errors (absent token still → `null`). The returned function carries `prime()` — pre-fetches JWKS via the verifier's `hydrate()`.

### `createLazyAccessToken({url, clientId, secret, fetch?}) -> {authorize, getToken}`

`authorize()` returns a cached unexpired token or fetches a fresh one; `getToken()` returns the current token without fetching. State is per-instance.

### `createRenewableAccessToken({url, clientId, secret, fetch?, retryInterval?, onError?}) -> {retrieveToken, cancelRenewal, getToken}`

`retrieveToken()` fetches a token and schedules an automatic refresh shortly before expiry (the timer is `unref`ed). A failed scheduled refresh keeps the previous token and retries every `retryInterval` ms (default 60000) until success or `cancelRenewal()`; failures go to `onError` (or the debug channel). `cancelRenewal(clearToken?)` stops the refresh/retry. Always read the live token via `getToken()`.

## Notes

- Verification posture (algorithm policy, JWKS rotation, claim checks) is aws-jwt-verify's; set `clientId` and `tokenUse` on the verifier.
- ESM-only with hand-written `.d.ts` sidecars; no build step. Frameworks are duck-typed at runtime (koa/express are not dependencies).
- v3 absorbed the sister packages `koa-cognito-middleware` / `cognito-express-middleware` (now frozen re-export thunks); their module-level guard statics became per-instance `makeAuth` bundles.
